title: Slow Cooker Beef Stroganoff
description: This comforting Slow Cooker Beef Stroganoff features tender chunks of beef chuck roast simmered with mushrooms and onions in a rich, creamy sauce. This hands-off American comfort food classic delivers deep flavors with minimal effort and tastes even better the next day.
ingredients: Freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 pounds beef chuck roast, well trimmed and cut into 1 1/2-inch chunks
instructions: Season 2 pounds beef chunks with 1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt, black pepper, and 1 tablespoon flour in a large bowl, tossing until evenly coated.

Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add 1 teaspoon olive oil. Sauté 1 chopped onion until softened and lightly golden, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer onions to the slow cooker and wipe the skillet clean.

Return skillet to stove and increase heat to medium-high. Spray generously with olive oil spray. Brown the seasoned beef chunks in batches, about 2 to 3 minutes per side until a deep crust forms. Transfer each batch to the slow cooker as it finishes.
Add 1 teaspoon sweet paprika, 1 cup beef broth, remaining 1 teaspoon kosher salt, and 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce to the slow cooker. Stir well to combine with onions and beef.
Top with 1 pound sliced mushrooms and gently stir. Cover and cook on HIGH for 5 hours or LOW for 8 to 10 hours, until beef is fork-tender and falls apart easily.

About 30 minutes before serving, temper 3/4 cup sour cream by stirring a few spoonfuls of hot cooking liquid into the sour cream in a small bowl, then stir the mixture back into the slow cooker. Set to LOW and cook 30 minutes more with lid on.

Cook 1 pound egg noodles according to package directions and drain well. Serve stroganoff over noodles or fold noodles into slow cooker just before serving. Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed.
notes: For gluten-free: use gluten-free flour, gluten-free Worcestershire sauce, and gluten-free noodles or serve over rice.
If using regular (not low-sodium) broth, reduce the remaining 1 teaspoon salt to 1/2 teaspoon and adjust at the end.
Store stroganoff and noodles separately to prevent noodles from getting mushy. Reheat gently over low heat, adding a splash of broth to loosen.
